channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How AI is empowering tech leaders
item.description: When it comes to IT procurement, “time is money” is an understatement. For many organizations, the procurement process eats up six to nine months — an expensive exercise in a time of digital transformation, when both tech agility and organizational leadership define business success.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China launches center to train 100-plus humanoid robots simultaneously
item.description: A humanoid robot training center has opened in Shanghai, a big step in China's efforts to become a global leader in robotics and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rumored iPhone 17 Pro Design Leaked, But Should You Believe It?
item.description: A new rumor suggests the iPhone 17 Pro could have a different design, but the source has a mixed track record and we're skeptical.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Buckle up for the supercharged Python interpreter
item.description: In this edition of our biweekly Python newsletter: The next version of Python brings an all-new variety of speed boost. Python packages may soon pack better labeling for their ingredients. Cython 3.1 tees up new features for converting Python to C.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Some Palo Alto Networks firewalls are spontaneously rebooting
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has acknowledged that some of its next-generation firewalls running the PAN-OS operating system are suddenly rebooting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck owner says he’s been threatened and harassed soon after Musk endorsed Trump
item.description: Dr. Kumait Jaroje said someone put a profane sticker on his gold Cybertruck. When he posted a photo of it on social media he told police he started getting threats online.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple Maps just got a lot better for cyclists in the UK – here’s what’s new
item.description: Apple Maps has supported bike directions in the UK for a long while, although the areas covered were very limited – but not anymore.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia systems could be facing another worrying security flaw
item.description: A bypass for a previous fix was recently discovered in Nvidia Container Toolkit and Nvidia GPU Operator.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: State says $400 million armored EV contract on hold
item.description: The State Department is placing a $400 million contract to build armored electric vehicles (EVs) on hold, a spokesperson told The Hill on Thursday. Tech billionaire Elon Musk’s Tesla appears to have been a likely recipient of the contract, which originated from the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Plans to spend $400 million on ‘Armored Teslas’ vanish from State Department procurement list
item.description: Elon Musk’s unprecedented role in Donald Trump’s administration as overseer of government spending has raised questions about how he might police himself when one of his companies competes for official contracts.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox host: Musk needs to be transparent about 'billions' in government contract
item.description: Fox News host Jessica Tarlov knocked billionaire Elon Musk over what she said is a lack of transparency he is displaying amid efforts to make widespread changes to the size of the federal government on President Trump's behalf.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Following Google, Apple’s maps now show Trump’s Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Apple renamed the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America on its maps Tuesday after an order by President Donald Trump was made official by the U.S. Geographic Names Information System.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Grimes rips Elon Musk for using their 4-year-old son as ‘a prop’ in Trump appearances
item.description: MSNBC host Lawrence O'Donnell also said that the little boy's presence in an Oval Office press conference with Trump Tuesday was 'a power play' by the tech billionaire.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: BBC: Chatbots distort the facts about news
item.description: It’s already known that today’s generative AI (genAI) tools often have trouble with basic facts. Now, it’s clear they don’t well with current events either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: VP calls for ideology-free AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple renames Gulf of Mexico as Gulf of America on maps
item.description: Apple Maps has renamed the Gulf of Mexico the Gulf of America, after the Trump administration updated the U.S. database for geographic names to reflect the president’s order renaming the body of water. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Enterprise tech spending to hit $4.9 trillion in 2025, driven by AI, cloud, and cybersecurity
item.description: Global enterprise technology spending is set to grow by 5.6% in 2025, reaching $4.9 trillion, as enterprises continue to prioritize investments in cybersecurity, cloud computing, generative AI, and digital transformation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top 20 apps tracking you every day
item.description: The apps collecting the most data about you are among the most widely used. Kurt the CyberGuy takes a look at 20 of these apps and how you can protect personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Never lose cell service again with this space-age satellite tech
item.description: AST SpaceMobile of Texas is developing the world's first global cellular broadband network that can connect directly to your smartphone without special equipment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok's US operations
item.description: Speaking virtually at a conference in Germany last month, Musk said he didn't envisage purchasing the social media app.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation
